$comment: First-load JS ceilings in kB (1000 bytes), gzipped — the same number `next build` prints in its First Load JS column. Enforced by `npm run perf:budget`; regenerate from a fresh build with `npm run perf:budget -- --update`. Raising a number is fine when the growth is deliberate, but do it in the PR that causes it so a reviewer sees the cost.
$defaultRouteKbComment: Applied to routes with no entry below, so a new page cannot land unbudgeted. Set from the current non-outlier ceiling; /traces, /shared/[token], /replay and /flows are heavier because they load the React Flow graph.
$tracesKbComment: Raised 224 -> 227 when the waterfall legend landed: the route measured 223.8 against a 224 ceiling, which passes but leaves 0.2 kB — close enough that ordinary chunk-splitting variance would fail CI on an unrelated change. Raised in the commit that caused the growth, per the note at the top.
